page: Google Cloud Platform x4-480-6t-metal is a bare-metal server instance belonging to the x4 server family. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 480 dedicated virtual central processing units (vCPUs) and a total memory capacity of 6144.0 GB. This configuration provides a memory-to-core ratio of 12.8 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server type does not include local NVMe storage or integrated graphics processing units (GPUs). All storage requirements must be met through external network-attached storage options. Additionally, the instance does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the massive memory footprint and high count of dedicated vCPUs, the x4-480-6t-metal is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and complex data analytics pipelines. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal deployment model eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing resource density and performance predictability for large-scale, memory-bound applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform x4-480-6t-metal is a bare-metal server designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, it features 480 dedicated vCPUs and 6144.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 12.8 GB of memory per core. The server does not include local storage or graphics processing units, meaning all storage needs must be handled via network-attached solutions. This hardware profile is optimized for massive in-memory databases, large-scale enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data analytics. By deploying on bare metal, the instance eliminates virtualization overhead, offering predictable performance and high resource efficiency for demanding, large-scale workloads.
